https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models.title: ChatGPT Models Explained: Complete Comparison Guide (2026)
https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models.text:
URL Source: https://www.ai-toolbox.co/chatgpt-models/chatgpt-models-explained-complete-comparison-2026

Published Time: 2026-01-06T00:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
**ChatGPT's 2026 lineup is built around the GPT-5 family: GPT-5.3 Instant (the default for every tier, including free), GPT-5.4 Thinking (paid tiers, deeper reasoning), and GPT-5.4 Pro (the highest-capability option for Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Edu).** A "Legacy Models" tab preserves GPT-5.2 Thinking for 90 days after the GPT-5.4 launch. Older models - GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.1 mini, o4-mini, and the original GPT-5 (Instant and Thinking) - were retired from ChatGPT on February 13, 2026 (OpenAI, 2026). ChatGPT now uses a single auto-switching router that picks Instant or Thinking based on the query. **ChatGPT Toolbox**, a Chrome extension with 18,000+ active users and a 4.5/5 Chrome Web Store rating, works with every current ChatGPT model and adds folders, full-text search, bulk export, prompt library, and prompt chaining to organize conversations across Instant, Thinking, and Pro modes.

OpenAI dramatically simplified the ChatGPT model picker in 2026. Where 2025 had a confusing mix of GPT-4o, GPT-4.1, GPT-4.5, o1, o3, o3-pro, and o4-mini, today there are three primary choices: **Instant**, **Thinking**, and **Pro**. Each maps to a specific capability tier in the GPT-5 family. This guide walks through every current model - capabilities, tier availability, context windows, thinking-time controls, and benchmarks - then gives you a task-first decision framework so you pick the right one every time. We'll close with real-world use cases by profession and answer the 8 questions people search most.

![Image 1: ChatGPT 2026 model lineup showing GPT-5.3 Instant, GPT-5.4 Thinking, and GPT-5.4 Pro](https://media.happyhourhub.co/ChatGPT-Toolbox-Media/blog88_1.webp)
## The 2026 ChatGPT Lineup: Only GPT-5 Family

**As of February 13, 2026, every active ChatGPT model belongs to the GPT-5 family. GPT-4 series and o-series models are retired.** OpenAI merged reasoning and general-purpose intelligence into a unified design, with one router (Instant) that can auto-switch to deeper reasoning (Thinking) when a query needs it.

The full list of retired models (Feb 13, 2026):

*   **GPT-4o** - the 2024-2025 flagship multimodal model (ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, and Edu keep GPT-4o in Custom GPTs until April 3, 2026)
*   **GPT-4.1** - the specialized coding model with a 1M token context window
*   **GPT-4.1 mini** - the lightweight version of GPT-4.1
*   **OpenAI o4-mini** - the small reasoning model in the o-series lineup
*   **GPT-5 Instant** and **GPT-5 Thinking** - the original GPT-5 models (August 2025)

API access to these retired models remains unchanged for developers with existing integrations, though OpenAI has not committed to a long-term API availability window. New projects should start on the current GPT-5.3 / GPT-5.4 models.

https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/te.title: The latest AI news we announced in March 2026
https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/te.text:
URL Source: https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/ai/google-ai-updates-march-2026/

Published Time: 2026-04-01T13:00:00+00:00

Markdown Content:
Apr 01, 2026

9 min read

Here’s a recap of our biggest AI updates from March 2026, including an expansion of Search Live, more ways to access Personal Intelligence and new tools that make it easier to switch to Gemini.

T

The Keyword Team

## General summary

In March, Google focused on making AI more helpful in your daily life with updates to Gemini. These updates help Gemini understand your context, turning devices into proactive helpers for work, creativity, and intuitive living. New features include expanded Search Live, enhanced AI tools in Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Drive, Google Maps upgrades, Personal Intelligence expansion, and tools to switch to Gemini.

Summaries were generated by Google AI. Generative AI is experimental.

## Bullet points

*   "The latest AI news we announced in March" highlights Google's AI advancements across various products.
*   Gemini got updates to better understand your context, turning devices into proactive, personalized helpers.
*   Google Maps was upgraded with Gemini, offering conversational help and a redesigned navigation experience.
*   New tools help you switch to Gemini, importing your chats and preferences from other AI apps.
*   Google is expanding AI's role in healthcare with funding, partnerships, and Fitbit health tracking updates.

Summaries were generated by Google AI. Generative AI is experimental.

## Basic explainer

Google had a busy March making AI more helpful. They updated Gemini to understand you better and help with daily tasks. Google Maps got smarter with AI, and they made it easier to switch to Gemini from other AI tools. They also added new AI features to Pixel phones and improved health tools with AI.

Summaries were generated by Google AI. Generative AI is experimental.

Your browser does not support the audio element.

[[duration]] minutes

For more than 20 years, we’ve invested in machine learning and AI research, tools and infrastructure to build products that make everyday life better for more people. Teams across Google are working on ways to unlock AI’s benefits in fields as wide-ranging as healthcare, crisis response and education. To keep you posted on our progress, we're doing a regular roundup of Google's most recent AI news.

Here’s a look back at some of our AI announcements from March.

https://ai.meta.com/blog/llama-4-multimo.text:
URL Source: https://ai.meta.com/blog/llama-4-multimodal-intelligence/

Markdown Content:
# The Llama 4 herd: The beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI innovation

*   [Products](https://ai.meta.com/blog/llama-4-multimodal-intelligence/#)
*   [AI Research](https://ai.meta.com/blog/llama-4-multimodal-intelligence/#)
*   [The Latest](https://ai.meta.com/blog/)
*   [About](https://ai.meta.com/blog/llama-4-multimodal-intelligence/#)
*   [Get Llama](https://www.llama.com/?utm_source=ai_meta_site&utm_medium=web&utm_content=AI_nav&utm_campaign=09252025_moment)
*   [Try Meta AI](https://applink.meta.ai/?utm_source=ai_meta_site&utm_medium=web&utm_content=AI_nav&utm_campaign=04082026_moment)
*   [](https://ai.meta.com/)

Large Language Model

# The Llama 4 herd: The beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI innovation

April 5, 2025•

12 minute read

![Image 2](https://scontent-sof1-1.xx.fbcdn.net/v/t39.2365-6/489528324_1866126614188079_2353760794201377773_n.png?_nc_cat=106&ccb=1-7&_nc_sid=e280be&_nc_ohc=y1j2xvjAYtIQ7kNvwEQ2iBs&_nc_oc=AdoNw09jR-LyoybJ9oHy9f6o4PDIkmK28eOqeIbR5GA8pk_Hdoz-pkQNjCwsNwxhHYzB6IDB6NP9P5G99KquuCFQ&_nc_zt=14&_nc_ht=scontent-sof1-1.xx&_nc_gid=UaEfICmiErnWlG4z56u18Q&_nc_ss=7a389&oh=00_Af1vKgbYPoe2jDxWlDhnyFn22FtTYgH3lRj-NoX_1hgurw&oe=69F6C273)

## Takeaways

*   We’re sharing the first models in the Llama 4 herd, which will enable people to build more personalized multimodal experiences.
*   Llama 4 Scout, a 17 billion active parameter model with 16 experts, is the best multimodal model in the world in its class and is more powerful than all previous generation Llama models, while fitting in a single NVIDIA H100 GPU. Additionally, Llama 4 Scout offers an industry-leading context window of 10M and delivers better results than Gemma 3, Gemini 2.0 Flash-Lite, and Mistral 3.1 across a broad range of widely reported benchmarks.
*   Llama 4 Maverick, a 17 billion active parameter model with 128 experts, is the best multimodal model in its class, beating GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 Flash across a broad range of widely reported benchmarks, while achieving comparable results to the new DeepSeek v3 on reasoning and coding—at less than half the active parameters. Llama 4 Maverick offers a best-in-class performance to cost ratio with an experimental chat version scoring ELO of 1417 on [LMArena](https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard).
*   These models are our best yet thanks to distillation from Llama 4 Behemoth, a 288 billion active parameter model with 16 experts that is our most powerful yet and among the world’s smartest LLMs. Llama 4 Behemoth outperforms GPT-4.5, Claude Sonnet 3.7, and Gemini 2.0 Pro on several STEM benchmarks. Llama 4 Behemoth is still training, and we’re excited to share more details about it even while it’s still in flight.
*   Download the Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick models today on [llama.com](https://www.llama.com/llama-downloads/) and [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/meta-llama).

Published Time: 2026-03-28T16:26:36+00:00

Markdown Content:
# Everything Claude Shipped in 2026: The Complete Guide

# [The AI Corner](https://www.the-ai-corner.com/)

Subscribe Sign in

# Everything Claude Has Shipped in 2026. And How to Actually Use It

### Anthropic shipped a major release roughly every two weeks since January. Here’s what’s live, what matters, and exactly how to set each thing up

[Ruben Dominguez](https://substack.com/@rubendominguez)

Mar 28, 2026

∙ Paid

93

7

Share

I’ll be honest with you.

Keeping up with Anthropic this year has been genuinely hard. A new release almost every day. A major one every two weeks. New models, new tools, new product categories that didn’t exist three months ago. If you took a few weeks off, you missed more than you think.

Everything Anthropic shipped in 52 days. February 1 to March 24, 2026. Product releases only. Most people caught five. Source: The Product Compass Newsletter, productcompass.pm

And this isn’t like missing a LinkedIn feature nobody uses. Claude impacts how you work. The people who’ve been paying attention have rebuilt entire workflows. The people who haven’t are still copy-pasting context into every new chat.

This is the guide I wish existed when I started. Everything that’s live as of March 28, 2026. How to set each thing up. When to use what. What’s actually worth your time.

Bookmark it. Come back to it. Share it with your team.

* * *

# The Models

## [Claude Opus 4.6](https://theaicorner1.substack.com/p/claude-opus-4-6-practical-guide)

Claude Opus 4.6 launched February 5, 2026. 1 million token context window, 78.3% on MRCR v2, and a 14.5 hour task completion window — the highest of any frontier model. Source: Anthropic.

The ceiling. Launched February 5 with a 1 million token context window.

*   **78.3% on MRCR v2 at 1M tokens** — highest among frontier models at that length

*   **14.5 hour task completion window** — longest of any frontier model

*   **$5/$25 per million tokens** on the API

*   **128K max output tokens**

*   Supports adaptive thinking with a new “max” effort level for peak capability

**Use [Opus 4.6](https://theaicorner1.substack.com/p/claude-opus-4-6-practical-guide) for:** complex analysis across large contexts, codebase refactoring, deep research, high-stakes deliverables, anything where quality matters more than cost.

**Don’t use [Opus](https://theaicorner1.substack.com/p/claude-opus-4-6-practical-guide) for:** anything you’ll run more than a few times a day. At $5/$25 per million tokens, a heavy Opus workflow can burn $50-100 per day. Default to Sonnet. Escalate to Opus only when Sonnet’s output isn’t good enough.

* * *

## Claude [Sonnet 4.6](https://www.the-ai-corner.com/p/anthropic-just-launched-a-030-ai?r=1krivi)

# Current Grok Version — April 2026 Latest xAI Model

The current Grok version is Grok 4.20 Beta 2. See features, access, and what changed in the latest xAI model.

By AIToolsRecap April 9, 2026 9 min read 429 views

![Image 2: Current Grok Version — April 2026 Latest xAI Model](https://aitoolsrecap.com/ArticleImages/current-grok-version-april-2026-xai-models-explained.jpg)

## What Is the Current Grok Version in April 2026?

**Grok 4.20 Beta 2** is the current version as of April 2026. It is available on X Premium+ and SuperGrok.

*   Version: Grok 4.20 Beta 2
*   Release: March 3, 2026
*   Access: X Premium+ / SuperGrok

## Latest Grok Updates (April 2026)

*   **Grok 4.2** — improved reasoning and faster response times
*   **Voice Mode** — now rolling out with real-time interaction
*   **Speed vs Quality Modes** — users can control performance vs cost
*   **Access** — limited to X Premium+ and SuperGrok tiers

**What this means:** Grok is evolving into a real-time AI assistant with better speed, control, and multimodal capabilities — competing directly with ChatGPT and Claude.

If you have searched for the current Grok version and found conflicting answers, you are not alone. xAI updates Grok frequently and runs multiple model variants simultaneously — each with different capabilities, context windows, and access tiers. This guide cuts through the confusion and tells you exactly what is available right now in April 2026.

https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-wee.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: Amazon Bedrock agent workflows, Amazon SageMaker private connectivity, and more (February 2, 2026) | Amazon Web Services
https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-wee.text:
URL Source: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-weekly-roundup-amazon-bedrock-agent-workflows-amazon-sagemaker-private-connectivity-and-more-february-2-2026/

Published Time: 2026-02-02T09:19:48-08:00

Markdown Content:
## [AWS News Blog](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/)

Over the past week, we passed [Laba festival](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Laba_Festival), a traditional marker in the Chinese calendar that signals the final stretch leading up to the Lunar New Year. For many in China, it’s a moment associated with reflection and preparation, wrapping up what the year has carried, and turning attention toward what lies ahead.

Looking forward, next week also brings [Lichun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lichun), the beginning of spring and the first of the 24 solar terms. In Chinese tradition, spring is often seen as the season when growth begins and new cycles take shape. There’s a common saying that “a year’s plans begin in spring,” capturing the idea that this is a time to set one’s direction and start fresh.

**Last week’s launches**

 Here are the launches that got my attention this week:

*   [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/) enhances support for agent workflows with server-side tools and extended prompt caching – Amazon Bedrock introduced two updates that improve how developers build and operate AI agents. [The Responses API now supports server-side tool use](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/01/amazon-bedrock-server-side-custom-tools-responses-api/), so agents can perform actions such as web search, code execution, and database updates within AWS security boundaries. [Bedrock also adds a 1-hour time-to-live (TTL) option for prompt caching](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/01/amazon-bedrock-one-hour-duration-prompt-caching/), which helps improve performance and reduce the cost for long-running, multi-turn agent workflows. Server-side tools are available with OpenAI GPT OSS 20B and 120B models, and the 1-hour prompt caching TTL is generally available for select Claude models by Anthropic in Amazon Bedrock.
*   [Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio adds private VPC connectivity with AWS PrivateLink](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2026/01/amazon-sagemaker-unified-studio-aws-privatelink/) – [Amazon SageMaker Unified Studio](https://aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/unified-studio/) now supports AWS PrivateLink, providing private connectivity between your VPC and SageMaker Unified Studio without routing customer data over the public internet.

https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.text:
URL Source: https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/

Markdown Content:
Meta unveiled Muse Spark on April 8, 2026, its first AI model from the newly formed Meta Superintelligence Labs. The natively multimodal model handles text, images, audio, and tools, competing with leading systems from OpenAI and Google on benchmarks like GPQA and HealthBench.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Model Capabilities and Reasoning Modes

Muse Spark features three reasoning modes: Instant for quick responses, Thinking for chain-of-thought on harder problems, and Contemplating, which deploys multiple agents in parallel to solve complex tasks faster than sequential methods. It supports visual chain-of-thought, allowing step-by-step reasoning on images, and multi-agent orchestration for tasks like trip planning with subagents handling itineraries and activities simultaneously.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") The model was trained with reinforcement learning that penalizes excessive thinking time, enabling efficient performance on math and science problems.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Benchmark Performance and Health Focus

On HealthBench Hard, Muse Spark achieved 42.8, surpassing GPT-5.4 at 40.1 and Gemini 3.1 Pro at 20.6, with training data curated by over 1,000 physicians for health reasoning like nutrition and drug information.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[4](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") It excels in visual STEM tasks, generating interactive displays and minigames, and performs strongly in multimodal reasoning under independent evaluation.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Deployment and Infrastructure Overhaul

https://samexpert.com/microsoft-copilot-.title: Microsoft's Copilot Reorganisation
https://samexpert.com/microsoft-copilot-.text:
URL Source: https://samexpert.com/microsoft-copilot-reorganisation-march-2026/

Published Time: 2026-03-27T17:25:34.612Z

Markdown Content:
## Microsoft's Copilot Reorganisation

On [17 March 2026](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/17/announcing-copilot-leadership-update/), Satya Nadella announced the most significant restructuring of Microsoft's AI organisation since the company [hired Mustafa Suleyman](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2024/03/19/mustafa-suleyman-deepmind-and-inflection-co-founder-joins-microsoft-to-lead-copilot/) two years earlier. Consumer and commercial Copilot were unified under one leadership team. Suleyman stepped back from day-to-day product leadership to focus on building in-house AI models, and a new generation of executives took the reins. Five days before that, Rajesh Jha, the Executive Vice President (EVP) who had led Microsoft's Experiences + Devices division for years, [announced his retirement](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/12/microsoft-announces-experiences-devices-leadership-changes/) after 35 years at the company.

Illustration: SAMexpert

These are structural changes to how Microsoft builds and delivers AI. They arrived against a backdrop of mixed adoption data, a tech stock downturn, [$37.5 billion in quarterly AI capital expenditure](https://www.theregister.com/2026/02/02/microsoft_ai_spend_copilot/), and a [partnership with OpenAI](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/10/28/the-next-chapter-of-the-microsoft-openai-partnership/) that has evolved from exclusivity towards mutual independence through four publicly renegotiated stages. Here is what happened, who the new leaders are, what their track records tell us about the direction ahead, and what it all means for the organisations that depend on Microsoft's products every day.

## Two separate announcements, five days apart

On [12 March 2026](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/12/microsoft-announces-experiences-devices-leadership-changes/), Microsoft announced that Rajesh Jha, Executive Vice President of Experiences + Devices, would retire after 35 years at the company. He will step down on 1 July 2026 and stay on in an advisory role. Jha had led the Experiences + Devices division responsible for [Microsoft 365 Copilot](https://samexpert.com/microsoft-365-copilot-licensing/), Windows, and Office. His responsibilities were divided among four EVPs reporting directly to Nadella: Perry Clarke, Charles Lamanna, Pavan Davuluri, and Ryan Roslansky. Jeff Teper was promoted to EVP, and Sumit Chauhan and Kirk Koenigsbauer were promoted to President. Jha's memo said the full cascade of organisational details would be finalised between March and June, ready for the start of FY27 (Microsoft's fiscal year 2027, beginning 1 July 2026).
